mnemonics: ACT DUMB: Actors (Users), Database, DBMS, Users, Metadata, Backup. Represents the main components of the DBMS environment.
keyPoints: The DBMS environment consists of hardware, software, data, procedures, and people.
The primary software components are the DBMS itself, the database, and application programs.
Users are categorized into end-users, application programmers, and database administrators (DBA).
commonMistakes: Confusing the Database Management System (DBMS) with the actual database itself.
Forgetting that people (users, programmers, DBA) are a critical component of the environment.
Overlooking the importance of metadata, which is data about the data stored in the database.
